user.title: Set up IP Ban Manager
user.description: Choose safe starting defaults. After setup, use the IP Ban Manager panel or Configure screen to manage live bans, blocked networks, default-deny mode, and notification behavior.

init.title: Manage IP Ban Manager
init.description: Manage the live allowlist and ban list. Submit applies changes immediately; no Home Assistant restart is needed.

confirm_clear_bans.description: This will remove {ban_count} blocked IP(s) from Home Assistant and rewrite ip_bans.yaml. Check the box to confirm.

ip_ban_disabled.title: Enable HTTP IP banning
ip_ban_disabled.description:
Home Assistant's built-in HTTP IP banning is currently disabled, so ban management and allowlist protection cannot run.

Set this key inside your existing `http:` section in `configuration.yaml`, then restart Home Assistant:

```yaml
http:
  ip_ban_enabled: true
```

integration_disabled_by_yaml.title: IP Ban Manager is disabled
integration_disabled_by_yaml.description:
The emergency YAML disable switch is enabled, so IP Ban Manager did not install its runtime hooks, panel, services, sensors, managed blocked networks, or default-deny handling.

When you are ready to use IP Ban Manager again, remove this key, then restart Home Assistant:

```yaml
ip_ban_manager: disabled
```

legacy_yaml_present.title: Remove old ban_allowlist YAML
legacy_yaml_present.description:
IP Ban Manager has already imported the old `ban_allowlist:` YAML settings into the UI config entry.

Remove the old `ban_allowlist:` block from `configuration.yaml`, then restart Home Assistant. The integration will keep using the imported UI settings.

legacy_folder_cleanup_failed.title: Legacy cleanup needs attention
